mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and lower financing stress, which lifts equity valuation multiples and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is strongest when CPIAUCSL is not accelerating.
failure_condition: Reject if falling FEDFUNDS is followed by negative 20d SPX returns in most episodes, or if the signal turns negative when CPIAUCSL inflation is stable to falling.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend marks rising recession risk and weaker household income growth, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month. The effect is triggered only when unemployment has materially broken higher.
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE breakouts above the stated threshold are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 40% of triggered instances, or if Rank IC stays near zero for any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in RSAFS and HOUST indicates broadening final demand and stronger housing-linked consumption, which should transmit to higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-HOUST signal does not outperform either series alone, or if positive joint moves are not associated with positive 60d SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO relative to its recent level signals stronger industrial activity and better earnings momentum, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The relationship fails when production rises only because of inventory rebuilding without demand confirmation.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive-month INDPRO increases are not followed by positive 20d SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal has no incremental predictive value after conditioning on RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is weak and PAYEMS is also soft, the market is pricing a broad demand slowdown that should pressure earnings and equity returns over the next quarter. The mechanism requires both real activity and payroll deterioration together.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-GDPC1/weak-PAYEMS condition does not produce negative 60d SPX returns in a clear majority of cases, or if either proxy alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A higher FEDFUNDS rate above a restrictive threshold tightens financial conditions and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect is strongest after a confirmed rate-hike regime.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive FEDFUNDS readings are not followed by negative 5d SPX returns at a rate materially above baseline, or if the sign flips positive during tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling PAYEMS and rising UNRATE indicates labor market deterioration, which should weaken consumer spending expectations and lower S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal is not active unless both labor series worsen together.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS declines and UNRATE increases do not predict negative 20d SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if the effect disappears once GDPC1 is included.

mechanism_narrative: A strong reading in RSAFS relative to the recent month signals resilient consumer demand and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next week. The effect fails if sales strength is concentrated in inflation rather than real volume.
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS breakout episodes do not yield positive 5d SPX returns more often than baseline, or if the signal vanishes when CPIAUCSL is accelerating sharply.
